NKJV.1: A Psalm of David. Bless the LORD, O my soul; And all that is within me, bless His holy name!
NKJV.2: Bless the LORD, O my soul, And forget not all His benefits:
NKJV.3: Who forgives all your iniquities, Who heals all your diseases,
NKJV.4: Who redeems your life from destruction,  Who crowns you with lovingkindness and tender mercies,
NKJV.5: Who satisfies your mouth with good things, So that your youth is renewed like the eagle’s.
NKJV.6: The LORD executes righteousness And justice for all who are oppressed.
NKJV.7: He made known His ways to Moses, His acts to the children of Israel.
NKJV.8: The LORD is merciful and gracious, Slow to anger, and abounding in mercy.
NKJV.9: He will not always strive with us, Nor will He keep His anger forever.
NKJV.10: He has not dealt with us according to our sins, Nor punished us according to our iniquities.
NKJV.11: For as the heavens are high above the earth, So great is His mercy toward those who fear Him;
NKJV.12: As far as the east is from the west, So far has He removed our transgressions from us.
NKJV.13: As a father pities his children, So the LORD pities those who fear Him.
NKJV.14: For He knows our frame; He remembers that we are dust.
NKJV.15: As for man, his days are like grass; As a flower of the field, so he flourishes.
NKJV.16: For the wind passes over it, and it is gone, And its place remembers it no more.
NKJV.17: But the mercy of the LORD is from everlasting to everlasting On those who fear Him, And His righteousness to children’s children,
NKJV.18: To such as keep His covenant, And to those who remember His commandments to do them.
NKJV.19: The LORD has established His throne in heaven, And His kingdom rules over all.
NKJV.20: Bless the LORD, you His angels, Who excel in strength, who do His word, Heeding the voice of His word.
NKJV.21: Bless the LORD, all you His hosts, You ministers of His, who do His pleasure.
NKJV.22: Bless the LORD, all His works, In all places of His dominion. Bless the LORD, O my soul!

KJV.1: A Psalm of David<S>1732</S>. Bless<S>1288</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>, O my soul<S>5315</S>: and all that is within<S>7130</S> me, bless his holy<S>6944</S> name<S>8034</S>.

KJV.8: The LORD<S>3068</S> is merciful<S>7349</S> and gracious<S>2587</S>, slow<S>750</S> to anger<S>639</S>, and plenteous<S>7227</S> in mercy<S>2617</S>. <sup>plentious: Heb. great in mercy</sup>
KJV.9: He will not always<S>5331</S> chide<S>7378</S>: neither will he keep<S>5201</S> his anger for ever<S>5769</S>.

KJV.11: For as the heaven<S>8064</S> is high above<S>1361</S> the earth<S>776</S>, so great<S>1396</S> is his mercy<S>2617</S> toward them that fear<S>3373</S> him. <sup>as: Heb. according to the height of the heaven</sup>
KJV.12: As far as<S>7368</S> the east<S>4217</S> is from the west<S>4628</S>, so far hath he removed<S>7368</S> our transgressions<S>6588</S> from us.
KJV.13: Like as a father<S>1</S> pitieth<S>7355</S> his children<S>1121</S>, so the LORD<S>3068</S> pitieth<S>7355</S> them that fear<S>3373</S> him.
KJV.14: For he knoweth<S>3045</S> our frame<S>3336</S>; he remembereth<S>2142</S> that we are dust<S>6083</S>.
KJV.15: As for man<S>582</S>, his days<S>3117</S> are as grass<S>2682</S>: as a flower<S>6731</S> of the field<S>7704</S>, so he flourisheth<S>6692</S>.
KJV.16: For the wind<S>7307</S> passeth over<S>5674</S> it, and it is gone; and the place<S>4725</S> thereof shall know<S>5234</S> it no more. <sup>it is: Heb. it is not</sup>
KJV.17: But the mercy<S>2617</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> is from everlasting<S>5769</S> to<S>5704</S> everlasting<S>5769</S> upon them that fear<S>3373</S> him, and his righteousness<S>6666</S> unto children's<S>1121</S> children<S>1121</S>;
KJV.18: To such as keep<S>8104</S> his covenant<S>1285</S>, and to those that remember<S>2142</S> his commandments<S>6490</S> to do<S>6213</S> them.
KJV.19: The LORD<S>3068</S> hath prepared<S>3559</S> his throne<S>3678</S> in the heavens<S>8064</S>; and his kingdom<S>4438</S> ruleth<S>4910</S> over all.
KJV.20: Bless<S>1288</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>, ye his angels<S>4397</S>, that excel<S>1368</S> in strength<S>3581</S>, that do<S>6213</S> his commandments<S>1697</S>, hearkening<S>8085</S> unto the voice<S>6963</S> of his word<S>1697</S>. <sup>that excel: Heb. mighty in strength</sup>

KJV.22: Bless<S>1288</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>, all his works<S>4639</S> in all places<S>4725</S> of his dominion<S>4475</S>: bless<S>1288</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>, O my soul<S>5315</S>.

ESV.1: Bless the LORD, O my soul, and all that is within me, bless his holy name!
ESV.2: Bless the LORD, O my soul, and forget not all his benefits,
ESV.3: who forgives all your iniquity, who heals all your diseases,
ESV.4: who redeems your life from the pit, who crowns you with steadfast love and mercy,
ESV.5: who satisfies you with good so that your youth is renewed like the eagle's.
ESV.6: The LORD works righteousness and justice for all who are oppressed.
ESV.7: He made known his ways to Moses, his acts to the people of Israel.
ESV.8: The LORD is merciful and gracious, slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love.
ESV.9: He will not always chide, nor will he keep his anger forever.
ESV.10: He does not deal with us according to our sins, nor repay us according to our iniquities.
ESV.11: For as high as the heavens are above the earth, so great is his steadfast love toward those who fear him;
ESV.12: as far as the east is from the west, so far does he remove our transgressions from us.
ESV.13: As a father shows compassion to his children, so the LORD shows compassion to those who fear him.
ESV.14: For he knows our frame;  he remembers that we are dust.
ESV.15: As for man, his days are like grass; he flourishes like a flower of the field;
ESV.16: for the wind passes over it, and it is gone, and its place knows it no more.
ESV.17: But the steadfast love of the LORD is from everlasting to everlasting on those who fear him, and his righteousness to children's children,
ESV.18: to those who keep his covenant and remember to do his commandments.
ESV.19: The LORD has established his throne in the heavens, and his kingdom rules over all.
ESV.20: Bless the LORD, O you his angels, you mighty ones who do his word, obeying the voice of his word!
ESV.21: Bless the LORD, all his hosts, his ministers, who do his will!
ESV.22: Bless the LORD, all his works, in all places of his dominion. Bless the LORD, O my soul!

NASB.1: [A Psalm] of David. Bless the LORD, O my soul, And all that is within me, [bless] His holy name.
NASB.2: Bless the LORD, O my soul, And forget none of His benefits;
NASB.3: Who pardons all your iniquities, Who heals all your diseases;
NASB.4: Who redeems your life from the pit, Who crowns you with lovingkindness and compassion;
NASB.5: Who satisfies your years with good things, [So that] your youth is renewed like the eagle.
NASB.6: The LORD performs righteous deeds And judgments for all who are oppressed.
NASB.7: He made known His ways to Moses, His acts to the sons of Israel.
NASB.8: The LORD is compassionate and gracious, Slow to anger and abounding in lovingkindness.
NASB.9: He will not always strive [with us], Nor will He keep [His anger] forever.
NASB.10: He has not dealt with us according to our sins, Nor rewarded us according to our iniquities.
NASB.11: For as high as the heavens are above the earth, So great is His lovingkindness toward those who fear Him.
NASB.12: As far as the east is from the west, So far has He removed our transgressions from us.
NASB.13: Just as a father has compassion on [his] children, So the LORD has compassion on those who fear Him.
NASB.14: For He Himself knows our frame; He is mindful that we are [but] dust.
NASB.15: As for man, his days are like grass; As a flower of the field, so he flourishes.
NASB.16: When the wind has passed over it, it is no more, And its place acknowledges it no longer.
NASB.17: But the lovingkindness of the LORD is from everlasting to everlasting on those who fear Him, And His righteousness to children's children,
NASB.18: To those who keep His covenant And remember His precepts to do them.
NASB.19: The LORD has established His throne in the heavens, And His sovereignty rules over all.
NASB.20: Bless the LORD, you His angels, Mighty in strength, who perform His word, Obeying the voice of His word!
NASB.21: Bless the LORD, all you His hosts, You who serve Him, doing His will.
NASB.22: Bless the LORD, all you works of His, In all places of His dominion; Bless the LORD, O my soul!

NLT.1:
Let all that I am praise the LORD;
with my whole heart, I will praise his holy name.
NLT.2:
Let all that I am praise the LORD;
may I never forget the good things he does for me.
NLT.3:
He forgives all my sins
and heals all my diseases.
NLT.4:
He redeems me from death
and crowns me with love and tender mercies.
NLT.5:
He fills my life with good things.
My youth is renewed like the eagle’s!
NLT.6:
The LORD gives righteousness
and justice to all who are treated unfairly.
NLT.7:
He revealed his character to Moses
and his deeds to the people of Israel.
NLT.8:
The LORD is compassionate and merciful,
slow to get angry and filled with unfailing love.
NLT.9:
He will not constantly accuse us,
nor remain angry forever.
NLT.10:
He does not punish us for all our sins;
he does not deal harshly with us, as we deserve.
NLT.11:
For his unfailing love toward those who fear him
is as great as the height of the heavens above the earth.
NLT.12:
He has removed our sins as far from us
as the east is from the west.
NLT.13:
The LORD is like a father to his children,
tender and compassionate to those who fear him.
NLT.14:
For he knows how weak we are;
he remembers we are only dust.
NLT.15:
Our days on earth are like grass;
like wildflowers, we bloom and die.
NLT.16:
The wind blows, and we are gone —
as though we had never been here.
NLT.17:
But the love of the LORD remains forever
with those who fear him.
His salvation extends to the children’s children
NLT.18:
of those who are faithful to his covenant,
of those who obey his commandments!
NLT.19:
The LORD has made the heavens his throne;
from there he rules over everything.
NLT.20:
Praise the LORD, you angels,
you mighty ones who carry out his plans,
listening for each of his commands.
NLT.21:
Yes, praise the LORD, you armies of angels
who serve him and do his will!
NLT.22:
Praise the LORD, everything he has created,
everything in all his kingdom.
Let all that I am praise the LORD.

WEB.1: By David. Praise Yahweh, my soul! All that is within me, praise his holy name!
WEB.2: Praise Yahweh, my soul, and don’t forget all his benefits;
WEB.3: who forgives all your sins; who heals all your diseases;
WEB.4: who redeems your life from destruction; who crowns you with loving kindness and tender mercies;
WEB.5: who satisfies your desire with good things, so that your youth is renewed like the eagle’s.
WEB.6: Yahweh executes righteous acts, and justice for all who are oppressed.
WEB.7: He made known his ways to Moses, his deeds to the children of Israel.
WEB.8: Yahweh is merciful and gracious, slow to anger, and abundant in loving kindness.
WEB.9: He will not always accuse; neither will he stay angry forever.
WEB.10: He has not dealt with us according to our sins, nor repaid us for our iniquities.
WEB.11: For as the heavens are high above the earth, so great is his loving kindness toward those who fear him.
WEB.12: As far as the east is from the west, so far has he removed our transgressions from us.
WEB.13: Like a father has compassion on his children, so Yahweh has compassion on those who fear him.
WEB.14: For he knows how we are made. He remembers that we are dust.
WEB.15: As for man, his days are like grass. As a flower of the field, so he flourishes.
WEB.16: For the wind passes over it, and it is gone. Its place remembers it no more.
WEB.17: But Yahweh’s loving kindness is from everlasting to everlasting with those who fear him, his righteousness to children’s children;
WEB.18: to those who keep his covenant, to those who remember to obey his precepts.
WEB.19: Yahweh has established his throne in the heavens. His kingdom rules over all.
WEB.20: Praise Yahweh, you angels of his, who are mighty in strength, who fulfill his word, obeying the voice of his word.
WEB.21: Praise Yahweh, all you armies of his, you servants of his, who do his pleasure.
WEB.22: Praise Yahweh, all you works of his, in all places of his dominion. Praise Yahweh, my soul!

and all that is within me, bless his holy name. ⓘ

AMP.1:
A Psalm of David. Bless and affectionately praise the LORD, O my soul,
And all that is [deep] within me, bless His holy name.
AMP.2:
Bless and affectionately praise the LORD, O my soul,
And do not forget any of His benefits;
AMP.3:
Who forgives all your sins,
Who heals all your diseases;
AMP.4:
Who redeems your life from the pit,
Who crowns you [lavishly] with lovingkindness and tender mercy;
AMP.5:
Who satisfies your years with good things,
So that your youth is renewed like the [soaring] eagle.
AMP.6:
The LORD executes righteousness
And justice for all the oppressed.
AMP.7:
He made known His ways [of righteousness and justice] to Moses,
His acts to the children of Israel.
AMP.8:
The LORD is merciful and gracious,
Slow to anger and abounding in compassion and lovingkindness.
AMP.9:
He will not always strive with us,
Nor will He keep His anger forever.
AMP.10:
He has not dealt with us according to our sins [as we deserve],
Nor rewarded us [with punishment] according to our wickedness.
AMP.11:
For as the heavens are high above the earth,
So great is His lovingkindness toward those who fear and worship Him [with awe-filled respect and deepest reverence].
AMP.12:
As far as the east is from the west,
So far has He removed our transgressions from us.
AMP.13:
Just as a father loves his children,
So the LORD loves those who fear and worship Him [with awe-filled respect and deepest reverence].
AMP.14:
For He knows our [mortal] frame;
He remembers that we are [merely] dust.
AMP.15:
As for man, his days are like grass;
Like a flower of the field, so he flourishes.
AMP.16:
For the wind passes over it and it is no more,
And its place knows it no longer.
AMP.17:
But the lovingkindness of the LORD is from everlasting to everlasting on those who [reverently] fear Him,
And His righteousness to children's children,
AMP.18:
To those who honor and keep His covenant,
And remember to do His commandments [imprinting His word on their hearts].
AMP.19:
The LORD has established His throne in the heavens,
And His sovereignty rules over all [the universe].
AMP.20:
Bless the LORD, you His angels,
You mighty ones who do His commandments,
Obeying the voice of His word!
AMP.21:
Bless the LORD, all you His hosts,
You who serve Him and do His will.
AMP.22:
Bless the LORD, all you works of His, in all places of His dominion;
Bless and affectionately praise the LORD, O my soul!

MSG.1:
A David psalm
O my soul, bless God.
From head to toe, I'll bless his holy name!
MSG.2:
O my soul, bless God,
don't forget a single blessing!
MSG.3:
He forgives your sins—every one.
He heals your diseases—every one.
MSG.4:
He redeems you from hell—saves your life!
He crowns you with love and mercy—a paradise crown.
MSG.5:
He wraps you in goodness—beauty eternal.
He renews your youth—you're always young in his presence.
MSG.6:
God makes everything come out right;
he puts victims back on their feet.
MSG.7:
He showed Moses how he went about his work,
opened up his plans to all Israel.
MSG.8:
God is sheer mercy and grace;
not easily angered, he's rich in love.
MSG.9:
He doesn't endlessly nag and scold,
nor hold grudges forever.
MSG.10:
He doesn't treat us as our sins deserve,
nor pay us back in full for our wrongs.
MSG.11:
As high as heaven is over the earth,
so strong is his love to those who fear him.
MSG.12:
And as far as sunrise is from sunset,
he has separated us from our sins.
MSG.13:
As parents feel for their children,
God feels for those who fear him.
MSG.14:
He knows us inside and out,
keeps in mind that we're made of mud.
MSG.15:
Men and women don't live very long;
like wildflowers they spring up and blossom,
MSG.16:
But a storm snuffs them out just as quickly,
leaving nothing to show they were here.
MSG.17:
God's love, though, is ever and always,
eternally present to all who fear him,
Making everything right for them and their children
MSG.18:
as they follow his Covenant ways
and remember to do whatever he said.
MSG.19:
God has set his throne in heaven;
he rules over us all. He's the King!
MSG.20:
So bless God, you angels,
ready and able to fly at his bidding,
quick to hear and do what he says.
MSG.21:
Bless God, all you armies of angels,
alert to respond to whatever he wills.
MSG.22:
Bless God, all creatures, wherever you are—
everything and everyone made by God.
And you, O my soul, bless God!

NIV.1:
Psalm 103
Of David.
Praise the Lord, O my soul;
all my inmost being, praise his holy name.
NIV.2:
Praise the Lord, O my soul,
and forget not all his benefits—
NIV.3:
who forgives all your sins
and heals all your diseases,
NIV.4:
who redeems your life from the pit
and crowns you with love and compassion,
NIV.5:
who satisfies your desires with good things
so that your youth is renewed like the eagle’s.
NIV.6:
The Lord works righteousness
and justice for all the oppressed.
NIV.7:
He made known his ways to Moses,
his deeds to the people of Israel:
NIV.8:
The Lord is compassionate and gracious,
slow to anger, abounding in love.
NIV.9:
He will not always accuse,
nor will he harbor his anger forever;
NIV.10:
he does not treat us as our sins deserve
or repay us according to our iniquities.
NIV.11:
For as high as the heavens are above the earth,
so great is his love for those who fear him;
NIV.12:
as far as the east is from the west,
so far has he removed our transgressions from us.
NIV.13:
As a father has compassion on his children,
so the Lord has compassion on those who fear him;
NIV.14:
for he knows how we are formed,
he remembers that we are dust.
NIV.15:
As for man, his days are like grass,
he flourishes like a flower of the field;
NIV.16:
the wind blows over it and it is gone,
and its place remembers it no more.
NIV.17:
But from everlasting to everlasting
the Lord’s love is with those who fear him,
and his righteousness with their children’s children—
NIV.18:
with those who keep his covenant
and remember to obey his precepts.
NIV.19:
The Lord has established his throne in heaven,
and his kingdom rules over all.
NIV.20:
Praise the Lord, you his angels,
you mighty ones who do his bidding,
who obey his word.
NIV.21:
Praise the Lord, all his heavenly hosts,
you his servants who do his will.
NIV.22:
Praise the Lord, all his works
everywhere in his dominion.
Praise the Lord, O my soul.

NRSVCE.1:
Bless the LORD, O my soul,
and all that is within me,
bless his holy name.
NRSVCE.2:
Bless the LORD, O my soul,
and do not forget all his benefits —
NRSVCE.3:
who forgives all your iniquity,
who heals all your diseases,
NRSVCE.4:
who redeems your life from the Pit,
who crowns you with steadfast love and mercy,
NRSVCE.5:
who satisfies you with good as long as you live
so that your youth is renewed like the eagle’s.
NRSVCE.6:
The LORD works vindication
and justice for all who are oppressed.
NRSVCE.7:
He made known his ways to Moses,
his acts to the people of Israel.
NRSVCE.8:
The LORD is merciful and gracious,
slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love.
NRSVCE.9:
He will not always accuse,
nor will he keep his anger forever.
NRSVCE.10:
He does not deal with us according to our sins,
nor repay us according to our iniquities.
NRSVCE.11:
For as the heavens are high above the earth,
so great is his steadfast love toward those who fear him;
NRSVCE.12:
as far as the east is from the west,
so far he removes our transgressions from us.
NRSVCE.13:
As a father has compassion for his children,
so the LORD has compassion for those who fear him.
NRSVCE.14:
For he knows how we were made;
he remembers that we are dust.
NRSVCE.15:
As for mortals, their days are like grass;
they flourish like a flower of the field;
NRSVCE.16:
for the wind passes over it, and it is gone,
and its place knows it no more.
NRSVCE.17:
But the steadfast love of the LORD is from everlasting to everlasting
on those who fear him,
and his righteousness to children’s children,
NRSVCE.18:
to those who keep his covenant
and remember to do his commandments.
NRSVCE.19:
The LORD has established his throne in the heavens,
and his kingdom rules over all.
NRSVCE.20:
Bless the LORD, O you his angels,
you mighty ones who do his bidding,
obedient to his spoken word.
NRSVCE.21:
Bless the LORD, all his hosts,
his ministers that do his will.
NRSVCE.22:
Bless the LORD, all his works,
in all places of his dominion.
Bless the LORD, O my soul.

NET.1: Praise the LORD, O my soul! With all that is within me, praise his holy name!
NET.2: Praise the LORD, O my soul! Do not forget all his kind deeds!
NET.3: He is the one who forgives all your sins, who heals all your diseases,
NET.4: who delivers your life from the Pit, who crowns you with his loyal love and compassion,
NET.5: who satisfies your life with good things, so your youth is renewed like an eagle’s.
NET.6: The LORD does what is fair, and executes justice for all the oppressed.
NET.7: The LORD revealed his faithful acts to Moses, his deeds to the Israelites.
NET.8: The LORD is compassionate and merciful; he is patient and demonstrates great loyal love.
NET.9: He does not always accuse, and does not stay angry.
NET.10: He does not deal with us as our sins deserve; he does not repay us as our misdeeds deserve.
NET.11: For as the skies are high above the earth, so his loyal love towers over his faithful followers.
NET.12: As far as the eastern horizon is from the west, so he removes the guilt of our rebellious actions from us.
NET.13: As a father has compassion on his children, so the LORD has compassion on his faithful followers.
NET.14: For he knows what we are made of; he realizes we are made of clay.
NET.15: A person’s life is like grass. Like a flower in the field it flourishes,
NET.16: but when the hot wind blows by, it disappears, and one can no longer even spot the place where it once grew.
NET.17: But the LORD continually shows loyal love to his faithful followers, and is faithful to their descendants,
NET.18: to those who keep his covenant, who are careful to obey his commands.
NET.19: The LORD has established his throne in heaven; his kingdom extends over everything.
NET.20: Praise the LORD, you angels of his, you powerful warriors who carry out his decrees and obey his orders!
NET.21: Praise the LORD, all you warriors of his, you servants of his who carry out his desires!
NET.22: Praise the LORD, all that he has made, in all the regions of his kingdom! Praise the LORD, O my soul!
